Safe Nonlinear Trajectory Generation for Parallel Autonomy With a Dynamic Vehicl
The Shared Control Dynamic Window Approach for Non-Holonomic Semiautonomous Robo
Novel Planning-Based Algorithms for Human Motion Prediction

Issues: Several entries use @misc while supplying journal/volume/pages/doi fields that indicate they are actually @article or @inproceedings/@incollection (e.g., dynamic_window_approach, shared_autonomy_drl, computational_models_intention, reinforcement_learning_parameterized).
At least one entry has an empty required field: author = {} (reinforcement_learning_parameterized), which will break many BibTeX styles and is incomplete bibliographically.
Non-BibTeX/LaTeX escaping present: journal = {IEEE Robotics & Automation Magazine} contains HTML entity '&' instead of LaTeX-safe '\&'.
Trailing commas after the last field in entries (e.g., after doi) are non-standard and can cause parse errors in some BibTeX processors.
Key naming is inconsistent and not very conventional (all lowercase with underscores; no author/year cues), increasing collision risk and reducing maintainability.

Likely wrong field usage for chapters/sections in edited volumes: using journal for a book/series title (e.g., Model-Based Reinforcement Learning; Springer Tracts in Advanced Robotics) suggests @incollection/@inbook with booktitle/editor/publisher is more appropriate.
The provided file content appears truncated mid-entry (user_adapted_shared_control), which would make the .bib file fail to compile as-is.
LLM evaluation.Comment: Overall structure is close to valid BibTeX, but multiple mis-typed @misc entries, an empty author field, HTML escaping, and at least one truncated/unfinished entry significantly reduce direct LaTeX usability. Many entries do include core fields (title/author/year/venue) and DOIs, but publication-type/field normalization is needed.

eval_report.comment: Good. Most papers covered with decent entry quality, room for improvement.
